qa_pairs.question: What is a Reference GPU Unit (RGU) and why was this unit introduced?
qa_pairs.answer: A Reference GPU Unit (RGU) is a unit introduced in the 2024 RAC year to rank all GPU models in production. It was brought in to alleviate problems that arose in the allocation process and during job execution when all GPUs were treated as equivalent for allocation purposes.

qa_pairs.question: How are Reference GPU Units (RGUs) calculated for different GPU models?
qa_pairs.answer: Reference GPU Units (RGUs) are calculated based on three evaluation criteria: FP32 score (40% weight), FP16 score (40% weight), and GPU memory score (20% weight). The NVidia A100-40gb GPU serves as the reference model, assigned an RGU value of 4.0, with its FP16, FP32, and memory performance each defined as 1.0. These weights and the reference model are used to determine RGU values for other GPU models.

qa_pairs.question: What is Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology and what does it enable for GPU resource scheduling?
qa_pairs.answer: Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology, introduced with the 2025 infrastructure renewal, makes it possible to schedule a fraction of a GPU. This allows different jobs, potentially belonging to different users, to run on the same physical GPU concurrently. A fraction of a GPU allocated to a single job is referred to as a GPU instance or MIG instance.
